title: argemone ochroleuca
intro: Argemone ochroleuca, also known as the yellow-flowered prickly poppy, is a perennial plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Argemone ochroleuca include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Argemone ochroleuca include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to promote overall well-being.
